The {{nihongo|'''Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun'''|私市円山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kisaichi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ayabe, Kyoto|en|Ayabe, Kyoto|WD=Q694370|lt=Ayabe, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1994.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138977 |title=私市円山古墳|trans-title= Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 70 meters and a higher of 10 meters. It is located north of the center of the Fukuchiyama basin, in the central area of former {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province}}, on the central reaches of the {{ill|Yura River (Japan)|en|Yura River (Japan)|WD=Q11577617|lt=Yura River}} at an elevation of 94 meters. It was discovered during a survey accompanying the construction of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|en|Kinki Expressway|WD=Q11638656|lt=Kinki Expressway}} (currently Maizuru Wakasa Expressway) in 1988. Until then, the existence of the tumulus was unknown. Due to this discovery, the construction method of the highway was changed from a cutting to a tunnel, and the Kofun was to be preserved. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] consisted of a central main room where the coffins were located, and sub-chambers to the north and south. Wooden coffins was found in the central and northern chambers, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} such as armor, iron spearheads, iron swords and other weapons, agricultural tools, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, and jewelry were excavated, along with a quiver containing 38 arrows. From the quality and quantity of the grave goods, it is assumed that the central burial was that of a king who ruled the middlereaches of the Yura River in the mid-fifth century, and the northern burials were possibly siblings, parents and children. Only agricultural tools were sub-burial on the south side, which is presumed to have been that of a servant.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The tumulus is about a two-kilometer walk from {{ill|Takatsu Station (Kyoto)|en|Takatsu Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q872015|lt=Takatsu Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kisaichi-Maruyama Kofun]][[en:Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:私市円山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kisaichi-Maruyama Kofun]][[en:Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:私市円山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kisaichi-Maruyama Kofun]][[en:Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:私市円山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Ayabe, Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun | native_name =私市円山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 私市円山古墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.314388888889|N|135.19638888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ayabe, Kyoto|en|Ayabe, Kyoto|WD=Q694370|lt=Ayabe, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|18|51.8|N|135|11|47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun'''|私市円山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kisaichi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ayabe, Kyoto|en|Ayabe, Kyoto|WD=Q694370|lt=Ayabe, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1994.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138977 |title=私市円山古墳|trans-title= Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 70 meters and a higher of 10 meters. It is located north of the center of the Fukuchiyama basin, in the central area of former {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province}}, on the central reaches of the {{ill|Yura River (Japan)|en|Yura River (Japan)|WD=Q11577617|lt=Yura River}} at an elevation of 94 meters. It was discovered during a survey accompanying the construction of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|en|Kinki Expressway|WD=Q11638656|lt=Kinki Expressway}} (currently Maizuru Wakasa Expressway) in 1988. Until then, the existence of the tumulus was unknown. Due to this discovery, the construction method of the highway was changed from a cutting to a tunnel, and the Kofun was to be preserved. The mound had a three-tiered structure, and river stones of the Yura River were used as ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were lined up on the flat part of the first and second tiers of the mound. In the [[archaeological excavation]], figurative ''haniwa'' and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery. including house-shaped and armor-shaped examples were excavated. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] consisted of a central main room where the coffins were located, and sub-chambers to the north and south. Wooden coffins was found in the central and northern chambers, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} such as armor, iron spearheads, iron swords and other weapons, agricultural tools, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, and jewelry were excavated, along with a quiver containing 38 arrows. From the quality and quantity of the grave goods, it is assumed that the central burial was that of a king who ruled the middlereaches of the Yura River in the mid-fifth century, and the northern burials were possibly siblings, parents and children. Only agricultural tools were sub-burial on the south side, which is presumed to have been that of a servant.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
When it was decided to preserve the tumulus, Ayabe City formulated the Kisaichi Maruyama tumulus environment improvement plan, re-laid the ''fukiishi'', lined up replicas of cylindrical ''haniwa'', and restored it as the "Maruyama Kofun Park" in May 1993.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In August 2014, heavy rains caused damage to the northwestern section of the mound, which collapsed over a width of 10 meters.
The tumulus is about a two-kilometer walk from {{ill|Takatsu Station (Kyoto)|en|Takatsu Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q872015|lt=Takatsu Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:私市円山古墳墳丘.JPG|Kofun from below File:私市円山古墳墳頂.JPG|Top of tumulus File:私市円山古墳造出し.JPG|View from top of tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kisaichi-Maruyama Kofun]][[en:Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:私市円山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kisaichi-Maruyama Kofun]][[en:Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:私市円山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kisaichi-Maruyama Kofun]][[en:Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:私市円山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Ayabe, Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun | native_name =私市円山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 私市円山古墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.314388888889|N|135.19638888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ayabe, Kyoto|en|Ayabe, Kyoto|WD=Q694370|lt=Ayabe, Kyoto|1=Ayabe, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|18|51.8|N|135|11|47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun'''|私市円山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kisaichi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ayabe, Kyoto|en|Ayabe, Kyoto|WD=Q694370|lt=Ayabe, Kyoto|1=Ayabe, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1994.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138977 |title=私市円山古墳|trans-title= Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 70 meters and a higher of 10 meters. It is located north of the center of the Fukuchiyama basin, in the central area of former {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}}, on the central reaches of the {{ill|Yura River (Japan)|en|Yura River (Japan)|WD=Q11577617|lt=Yura River|1=Yura River (Japan)}} at an elevation of 94 meters. It was discovered during a survey accompanying the construction of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|en|Kinki Expressway|WD=Q11638656|lt=Kinki Expressway|1=Kinki Expressway}} (currently Maizuru Wakasa Expressway) in 1988. Until then, the existence of the tumulus was unknown. Due to this discovery, the construction method of the highway was changed from a cutting to a tunnel, and the Kofun was to be preserved. The mound had a three-tiered structure, and river stones of the Yura River were used as ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were lined up on the flat part of the first and second tiers of the mound. In the [[archaeological excavation]], figurative ''haniwa'' and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery. including house-shaped and armor-shaped examples were excavated. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] consisted of a central main room where the coffins were located, and sub-chambers to the north and south. Wooden coffins was found in the central and northern chambers, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} such as armor, iron spearheads, iron swords and other weapons, agricultural tools, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and jewelry were excavated, along with a quiver containing 38 arrows. From the quality and quantity of the grave goods, it is assumed that the central burial was that of a king who ruled the middlereaches of the Yura River in the mid-fifth century, and the northern burials were possibly siblings, parents and children. Only agricultural tools were sub-burial on the south side, which is presumed to have been that of a servant.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
When it was decided to preserve the tumulus, Ayabe City formulated the Kisaichi Maruyama tumulus environment improvement plan, re-laid the ''fukiishi'', lined up replicas of cylindrical ''haniwa'', and restored it as the "Maruyama Kofun Park" in May 1993.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In August 2014, heavy rains caused damage to the northwestern section of the mound, which collapsed over a width of 10 meters.
The tumulus is about a two-kilometer walk from {{ill|Takatsu Station (Kyoto)|en|Takatsu Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q872015|lt=Takatsu Station|1=Takatsu Station (Kyoto)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:私市円山古墳墳丘.JPG|Kofun from below File:私市円山古墳墳頂.JPG|Top of tumulus File:私市円山古墳造出し.JPG|View from top of tumulus </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun in Ayabe, Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun | native_name =私市円山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 私市円山古墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.314388888889|N|135.19638888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ayabe, Kyoto|en|Ayabe, Kyoto|WD=Q694370|lt=Ayabe, Kyoto|1=Ayabe, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|18|51.8|N|135|11|47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun'''|私市円山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kisaichi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ayabe, Kyoto|en|Ayabe, Kyoto|WD=Q694370|lt=Ayabe, Kyoto|1=Ayabe, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1994.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138977 |title=私市円山古墳|trans-title= Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kisaichi Maruyama Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 70 meters and a higher of 10 meters. It is located north of the center of the Fukuchiyama basin, in the central area of former {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}}, on the central reaches of the {{ill|Yura River (Japan)|en|Yura River (Japan)|WD=Q11577617|lt=Yura River|1=Yura River (Japan)}} at an elevation of 94 meters. It was discovered during a survey accompanying the construction of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|en|Kinki Expressway|WD=Q11638656|lt=Kinki Expressway|1=Kinki Expressway}} (currently Maizuru Wakasa Expressway) in 1988. Until then, the existence of the tumulus was unknown. Due to this discovery, the construction method of the highway was changed from a cutting to a tunnel, and the Kofun was to be preserved. The mound had a three-tiered structure, and river stones of the Yura River were used as ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were lined up on the flat part of the first and second tiers of the mound. In the [[archaeological excavation]], figurative ''haniwa'' and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery. including house-shaped and armor-shaped examples were excavated. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] consisted of a central main room where the coffins were located, and sub-chambers to the north and south. Wooden coffins was found in the central and northern chambers, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} such as armor, iron spearheads, iron swords and other weapons, agricultural tools, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and jewelry were excavated, along with a quiver containing 38 arrows. From the quality and quantity of the grave goods, it is assumed that the central burial was that of a king who ruled the middlereaches of the Yura River in the mid-fifth century, and the northern burials were possibly siblings, parents and children. Only agricultural tools were sub-burial on the south side, which is presumed to have been that of a servant.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
When it was decided to preserve the tumulus, Ayabe City formulated the Kisaichi Maruyama tumulus environment improvement plan, re-laid the ''fukiishi'', lined up replicas of cylindrical ''haniwa'', and restored it as the "Maruyama Kofun Park" in May 1993.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In August 2014, heavy rains caused damage to the northwestern section of the mound, which collapsed over a width of 10 meters.
The tumulus is about a two-kilometer walk from {{ill|Takatsu Station (Kyoto)|en|Takatsu Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q872015|lt=Takatsu Station|1=Takatsu Station (Kyoto)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:私市円山古墳墳丘.JPG|Kofun from below File:私市円山古墳墳頂.JPG|Top of tumulus File:私市円山古墳造出し.JPG|View from top of tumulus </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Kisaichi-Maruyama Kofun}} *[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/gallery/iseki/gallery_file/g-i002.html Kyoto Prefecture Research Center for Archaeological Properties] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.ayabe-kankou.net/spot/kisaichimaruyamakofun/ Ayabe City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=3804&r=1650156977.5535 Kyoto Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}
